hypothesis_text: Welcome to Investors' Annual Accounts 2021 presentation. We are glad that you are joining us here today and you can join us over the phone or over the web on our website investorab.com. We will soon hear our CEO, Yuann Foshell and CFO, Helena Saxon, present the annual accounts. And after that we will have a Q&A session and you can post your questions then over the phone as usual or also in the chat on the web. One welcome and by that I hand over to you, Yuann. Thank you, Vivek and welcome to this courtly result presentation. So if we start on the first page, I think we, despite all the challenges that we had during the past year, we did in fact see a gradual improvement of the global economy during the year. And of course that in combination with the low interest rate environment was also supported the stock market. And the strong equity market of course benefited investors, but even adjusting for that I think it's fair to say that 2021 was a very strong year for investors. During the year our net asset value grew by more than 40% and our total share in the return was up 55% and that can be compared with the stock market in Sweden being up 39%. And if we look on the corresponding figure for the fourth quarter our net asset value was up 12% and our total share in the return was up 21% and that can be compared with the stock market being up 12%. Importantly I think that overall that our companies are delivering good operational performance. They have been able to really manage the challenges out there not the least the pandemic and the supply chain related challenges. On the listed companies a number of the companies have made important strategic acquisitions and investments during the year within Patricia, a number of the companies have made sizable add on acquisition and I will come back to that later on. The investment in equity develops wrongly during the year and was up more than 100% and the cash flow at 4.8 billion was the second best in the history. We sharpen our climate targets and we are continuously putting higher for the insustainability, putting in our valuation plans and especially of course an extra focus is on the energy transitions that affect the market.
